summary: Retire the requirement `package` facet and membership `source_package` from the lock rows (drift.rs) and the member-document codec (document.rs)

per.section: Decision: clause arrays and two quantifiers, not a package noun

edit.description: Delete `RequirementRow.package` (drift.rs:986) and `MembershipRow.source_package` (drift.rs:1034) and their table write/read: the `package` column write (drift.rs:1269-1270) + read (drift.rs:1295), the `source_package` write (drift.rs:1386-1387) + read (drift.rs:1398). RequirementRow keeps name/kind/required + the set-scope facets; MembershipRow drops only its conformance column. drift reads columns via serde `#[serde(default)]` (no `deny_unknown_fields`), so an SDK still emitting `package`/`source_package` is silently ignored — safe to drop the Rust column ahead of the SDK cut (PKG-NOUN-SDK-COLUMN). Leave the drift.rs:190 `frozen`/'package-fetch step' comment — that names the floor-package network step, a different noun.

Delete `PublishedRequirement.package` (document.rs:272) and its parse (document.rs:466,473,488) and emit (`add_requirement`, document.rs:323-324). Rewrite the doc comments enumerating the published facets as `means`/`kind`/`package`/`required` (document.rs:102,110,121,257,312) to `means`/`kind`/`required`. Fix the four `package: Some(...)`/`package: None` literals in this file's fixtures (document.rs:758,768,790,797). `main.rs::to_requirement` already ignores `published.package` (compose::Requirement carries no package field), so no lift change.

Comment-only: fix the stale doc comment on `union_published_requirements` (main.rs:1032) — a member header now publishes `means`/`kind`/`required`, not `means`/`kind`/`package`/`required`. No code change. (Serialized ahead of REACH-DIAL-RETIRE's main.rs edit, so no parallel conflict.)

Remove the `package: None`/`source_package: None` fields from the RequirementRow/MembershipRow literals in the payload builders (:136,:147,:156) and drop any assertion pinning the package/source_package column round-trip.

Drop `package: None` from both the `PublishedRequirement` builder (:132) and the `RequirementRow` builder (:180), and `source_package: None` from the MembershipRow literal (:507). This file carries literals of all three structs — the reason the drift and document cuts are one entry, not two parallel `open`s.
Drop the `package: None` field from the RequirementRow literal (:97) — compiler-flagged.
Drop the `package: None` field from the RequirementRow literal (:221); the real-SDK-subprocess byte-reproduction assertion is unaffected (the SDK never emitted the column).
Drop the `package: None` field from the RequirementRow literal (:61) — compiler-flagged.
Drop the `package: None` field from the RequirementRow literal (:160) — compiler-flagged.

Drop the `package: None` field from the PublishedRequirement literal (:68). The `why` snapshots naming a 'Governing package' are floor-package (std-lib) narration, not the requirement facet — leave them untouched.

tests.asserts: the requirement/membership declaration rows round-trip with no package/source_package column

acceptance: `rg -n "package|source_package" src/drift.rs src/document.rs` returns nothing requirement-facet-related (only drift.rs:190's floor-package-fetch comment survives); no `package: None`/`source_package: None` literals remain across tests/; cargo test green.
notes: Folds the former PKG-NOUN-LOCK-ROWS. The requirement `package` facet lives in two Rust homes — drift's lock rows and document.rs's member-document codec — and tests/requirement_roster.rs carries both plus the MembershipRow literal, so they can't split into parallel `open`s. PKG-NOUN-ENGINE-RETIRE (shipped 5ed652f) cut compose/roster; this is the lock+codec tail. The floor-`package` std-lib (PackageResolver, `packages/` tree) is a separate human-gated demolition.

summary: Drop the dead SDK `RequirementRow.package` field — never populated by `requirementRows`

per.section: Decision: clause arrays and two quantifiers, not a package noun

edit.description: Delete `RequirementRow.package` (declarations.ts:39). `requirementRows` already emits only name/kind/required/verified_by — the `package?` field is dead type surface, never written. No SEAM_VERSION bump: the wire payload is unchanged (the field was never emitted). Update the RequirementRow TSDoc that lists 'kind/package/required'.

acceptance: `rg -n "package" sdk/src/declarations.ts` returns nothing requirement-facet-related; `pnpm --dir sdk test` (tsc + node --test) green; tests/emit.rs (real SDK subprocess) still byte-reproduces the lock.
notes: Third chain link. Disjoint file (sdk/) but serialized after the Rust facet cut to keep the seam coherent and avoid churning declarations.ts under REACH-DIAL-RETIRE, which follows.

summary: Retire the reachability dial — the assembly `reachability` fact and its severity opt-in; 40-composition: there is no fifth field

per.section: Decision: the reachability dial retires into the clause algebra

edit.description: Delete `reachability_from_declarations` (main.rs:1141) and its call (main.rs:567), and the `if let Some(reachability) = assembly_reachability { … }` opt-in block that builds the activation map and calls `graph::reachable` (main.rs:726-752). 40-composition: reachability is demanded by an ordinary edge-scope clause, not a dedicated field. Keep `pub fn graph::reachable` (a live capability, still exercised by tests/graph.rs) — only its dial-driven opt-in goes. Sweep the dial's narrating comments (main.rs:554,720-726).

Delete the `Harness.reachability` field — the `reachability?: Severity` on the assembly value (assembly.ts:35), its doc line (:34), and the init passthrough (:48,:55). The assembly is four fields: members · expect · require · settings. Fix the header TSDoc naming a fifth field (assembly.ts:4).

Delete the `if (harness.reachability !== undefined) facts.push({ fact: "reachability", … })` emission in `assemblyFactRows` (declarations.ts:152-154) and the doc line naming 'reachability when the dial is set' (declarations.ts:147). The AssemblyFactRow shape stays (authority + edge facts survive). No SEAM_VERSION bump — dropping an optional fact the Rust reader now ignores.

This suite drives the code-locus dial (`fact = "reachability"` golden lock + `[reachability]` opt-in). Delete it — the dial it exercises is retired. Reachability's library machinery stays proven by tests/graph.rs's `reachability` module (untouched); directive_classing.rs/read_verbs.rs only mention reachability in prose, unaffected.

tests.asserts: the `reachable` library predicate still holds over synthetic graphs (capability retained; only the assembly dial retired)

acceptance: `rg -n "reachability_from_declarations|assembly_reachability|fact.*reachability" src/ sdk/src` returns nothing; the assembly is four fields; cargo test + `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Chain tail — shares main.rs with the facet cut's doc-comment sweep and declarations.ts + assembly.ts with PKG-NOUN-SDK-COLUMN, hence serialized. Reachability-as-edge-scope-clause (the corpus replacement, 40-composition) rides the clause-algebra growth downstream of REQUIREMENT-CLAUSES-RECUT — deferred, not this cut.

summary: Recut the requirement to `means·kind·required·clauses?·verifiedBy?` — retire the count/unique/membership/degree facet spelling for a clauses array, across engine + SDK + seam

per.section: Decision: set-scope demands are clauses — no noun enumerates the algebra

gate.reason: frame-scale and cross-language: the four facets live in compose.rs (Requirement + CountBound/Membership/DegreeBound), drift.rs RequirementRow columns, roster.rs (the set-scope check), AND sdk/contract.ts (:83-86), and moving them onto a `clauses` array reshapes ClauseRow/engine dispatch to carry set/edge-scope predicates and bumps SEAM_VERSION on both sides (tests/emit.rs runs the real SDK). Needs a decomposition ceremony (John + session) to cut the serialized chain before parallel-safe slices, like GATE-READ-LOCK-DEMOLITION got. Also resolves whether reachability-as-clause and the held general-degree-band predicate ride this same clause-algebra growth.

edit.description: The Requirement interface enumerates `count?`/`unique?`/`membership?`/`degree?` (contract.ts:83-86) and lacks `clauses?` — the facet spelling 10-contracts rejects. Placeholder anchor; the ceremony scopes the full cross-language chain (compose.rs/drift.rs/roster.rs/engine.rs + sdk/contract.ts/declarations.ts + SEAM_VERSION).

acceptance: A requirement carries set-/edge-scope demands as ordinary clause values (each with its own severity/guidance/cite), not named facet fields; the SDK Requirement type reads `means·kind·required·clauses?·verifiedBy?`; the lock's requirement row carries no facet columns; SEAM_VERSION bumped in lockstep.
notes: Parked for a decomposition ceremony — the inbox's 'disjoint sdk entry' framing under-scopes it (the Rust facets in compose/drift/roster + the SEAM bump make it a serialized chain). Do not file the naive single entry; the under-scoping is the thing to surface. Downstream of it: reachability-as-edge-scope-clause (the REACH-DIAL replacement).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
